name: boolExpr
description: Returns a boolean value from evaluating a condition using operators (==, !=, ===, !==, deepEqual, notDeepEqual, <, <=, >, >=, &&, ||, isNull, isNotNull, !). Always returns a boolean (true or false). == and != use JS loose equality (e.g. 0 == "" is true). === and !== use JS strict equality (no type coercion). deepEqual and notDeepEqual use recursive structural equality (objects and arrays are compared by value, not by reference; equivalent to fast-deep-equal). isNull/isNotNull check for null or undefined (JS loose equality). ! applies JS boolean NOT semantics (any falsy value: null, undefined, 0, false, ""). && and || use JS truthiness on left and right operands. Unary operators (isNull, isNotNull, !) do not use the right operand. Unlike ifThenElse, there are no 'then' or 'else' branches. In SQL (PostgreSQL), === and deepEqual both map to = on jsonb (which is structural equality); notDeepEqual and !== both map to <>.
